mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4.5% signals tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low UNRATE regimes are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong economic growth that drives equity earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in high GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low INDPRO regimes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in CPI (CPIAUCSL) above 2% and falling Fed funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates stagflationary pressures that create negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in residential construction (HOUST) below 1,100k units signals weak housing demand that reduces equity valuations, producing negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in low HOUST regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k and falling unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% indicates a strong labor market that drives positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in strong labor regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in money supply (RSAFS) above 5% combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates monetary overreach that causes negative SPX returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in monetary excess regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 3-year window.
